title: AbbVie's pursuit of Parkinson's back in the pink, buying mitochondria biotech for $110M
summary: AbbVie has acquired Mitokinin for $110 million upfront, with up to $445 million additional in milestone payments, bringing the biotech's PINK1 activator into its neuroscience pipeline. The therapy aims to treat Parkinson's disease by enhancing the body's ability to clear damaged mitochondria and mitigate neurodegeneration-linked inflammation. This marks another aggressive move by AbbVie to build out its Parkinson's portfolio after recent setbacks with other candidates and partnerships.
